description: File transfer from a Server to a Device
doc: Request a file transfer operation. Providing a URL from which the device will download a file with an HTTP GET request, the destination of the file to download is implementation specific.

mappings.doc: If the file is to be stored, this MUST be unique to the file (e.g. hash of the content). For a file that needs to be streamed, it can be unique for the single request (e.g. uuid).

mappings.description: The URL to get the file from
Keys for the HTTP headers, must be in the order of the values
Values for the HTTP headers, must be in the order of the keys
Encoding of the file or directory to download
mappings.doc: Optional enum string for the file encoding with default value empty, other values are: [gz, lz4, tar, tar.gz, tar.lz4]
Total file size (if multiple files) uncompressed in bytes. It's used to reserve this space on the device.

mappings.description: Flag to enable the progress reporting of the download.

mappings.doc: Must be in the form sha256:deadbeaf, and should be used only if supported by the device.

mappings.description: TTL on how long to keep the file for
mappings.doc: Optional ttl for how long to keep the file for, if 0 is forever
Optional unix mode for the file, set to default if 0. All files are immutable, so setting it to writable has no effect.

mappings.description: Uid of the user owning the file
mappings.doc: Optional unix uid of the user owning the file, set to default if -1.

mappings.description: Gid of the user owning the file
mappings.doc: Optional unix gid of the group owning the file, set to default if -1.

mappings.description: Destination where the file should be written to.
mappings.doc: String enum specifying the destination type, with allowed values: [storage, streaming, filesystem].

mappings.description: Destination-specific information on where to write the file to.
mappings.doc: The value depends on the selected destination type: for 'storage' and 'streaming' it's an empty string, and for 'filesystem' is a path to a file on the device.
